name: Pigeon Forge Pintos
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the pinto beans and discard any imperfect ones.
2. Cover the beans with water and bring to a boil.
3. Boil for 2 minutes, then soak the beans for at least 1 hour or longer.
4. Drain the beans (do not drain the cooking liquid).
5. Cover the salt pork with boiling water. Let stand for 2 minutes, then drain.
6. Make 1-inch gashes every half inch in the pork without cutting through the rind.
7. Place the beans in a bean pot and push the pork down into the beans until all but the rind is covered.
8. Mix together the remaining ingredients: molasses, dry mustard, and brown sugar.
9. Add 1 cup of the reserved cooking water and bring to a boil.
10. Pour the mixture over the beans and add enough water to cover the beans.
11. Cover the bean pot and bake at 250°F for 6 to 8 hours.
12. Add more water as needed to keep the beans moist throughout the baking process.
13. Uncover the pot during the last hour of baking to allow the rind to brown and become crisp.
